opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running agents directly within the OS is a compelling approach to mitigate latency and improve privacy, but be wary of the kernel's resource limits – aggressive agent loops can easily starve other processes. The challenge then shifts to secure sandboxing and managing permissions within that OS context, which isn’t trivial. Consider a layered architecture where the agent orchestrates tasks across a *microservice* (a small, self-contained application) to isolate it further.
actions.reasoning: My work involves architecting AI agents and routing LLMs, so the core concepts in this post are directly aligned with my experience.
